description: Prove that splitting a list produces halves whose lengths are both at most the length of the original list.
preamble:
def splitList (lst : List α) : (List α × List α) :=
  match lst with
  | [] => ([], [])
  | x :: xs =>
    let (a, b) := splitList xs
    (x :: b, a)
statement:
theorem splitList_shorter_le (lst : List α) :
    (splitList lst).fst.length ≤ lst.length ∧
      (splitList lst).snd.length ≤ lst.length
notes: From fp-lean examples/Examples/ProgramsProofs/Inequalities.lean lines 563-567. Ground-truth proof: fun_induction splitList <;> grind
